title: Lefties Losing It: ‘Sadistic’ Oscar attendees demand ceasefire in Iran
description: Sky News host Rita Panahi has labelled an Oscar attendee “sadistic” after they demanded a ceasefire in Iran.

title: Israeli military begins targeted ground operation ‘deeper’ into southern Lebanon
description: Sky News host Sharri Markson says Israel is launching a targeted ground operation deeper into southern Lebanon in response to continued Hezbollah attacks.

title: ‘No surprise there’: Starmer ‘reluctant’ to back US over fear of voter backlash
description: Sky News host Andrew Bolt wonders why Britain isn’t helping the US protect its oil tankers that Iran is attacking in the Persian Gulf.   “Well, no surprise, but the first reaction of Britain’s prime minister is to say no,” Mr Bolt said.   “Why is Keir Starmer so reluctant to help the US?  “Is it because he is so scared of Britain’s big Muslim minority?”

title: ‘Shocking’: Scenes at British anti-Israel protest spreads high level of ‘extremism’
description: ‘Triggered with Samara Gill’ host Samara Gill discusses her experience at an anti-Israel protest in England.   “It was shocking,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.   “I cannot believe that England has let this level of extremism into the country.  “It’s dictating foreign policy massively.”

title: ‘They’ve gotten the memo’: Oscars 2026 praised for ‘muted’ political commentary
description: Sky News contributor Kosha Gada says the Oscars have taken a “step in the right direction” with the low political commentary this year.   “They were a little bit restrained this year relative to what they’ve been in the past,” Ms Gada told Sky News host Sharri Markson.   “It feels like they’ve finally sort of gotten the memo that people don’t want to hear political commentary from our entertainers.   “It was genuinely pretty muted.”

title: Conan O’Brien makes jab at Andrew’s arrest at 2026 Oscars
description: Sky News contributor Kosha Gada talks about some of the blows taken at the Oscars.    “Conan O'Brien made a jab at the fact that no Brits were nominated for anything,” Ms Gada told Sky News host Sharri Markson.   “He made a jab at former prince Andrew and the arrest.  “That was something that got some gasps from the audience, followed by laughter.”

title: ‘Enormous spin machine’: Poll suggests voters aren’t buying government’s cost-of-living defence
description: Sky News host Paul Murray reacts to the results of an opinion poll which shows that voters blame Prime Minister Anthony Albanese for the cost-of-living crisis and not the war in the Middle East.   “We have been pointing this out for a long time, the enormous spin machine that saw the federal government being able to pretend that when interest rates went up it was someone else’s fault and when it went down it was their responsibility,” Mr Murray said.   “A sign of the dam wall potentially breaking in the BS that we are fed by the government.  “The biggest group by a country mile say, ‘who do you hold responsible for rising living costs’, the federal government.”

title: The winners of the Oscars revealed
description: Sky News contributor Kosha Gada discusses the “top two winners” of the Oscars.   “One Battle After Another is the one that swept most of the awards,” Ms Gada told Sky News host Sharri Markson.   “Sinners was sort of the second place.  “Hollywood loves to explore those racial themes.”

title: ‘He still hasn’t done it’: Taylor blasts Bowen for his lack of knowledge on fuel security
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor gives advice to the Labor government on what they can be doing differently for fuel security.   “Let the Ampol Refinery sell their fuel in Australia,” Mr Taylor told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “They are being forced by Labor’s emission standards to export their fuel right now.   “They should be selling it in Australia.   “Bowen told us he was going to do it; he still hasn’t done it.  “We need that fuel where it is most needed right now.”

title: ‘She’s fantastic’: Angus Taylor backs Liberal candidate for Farrer Raissa Butkowski
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor boasts about the “fantastic” Liberal candidate for Farrer Raissa Butkowski.   Mr Taylor told Sky News host Paul Murray she is “really unique and extraordinary’.   “She’s … deeply immersed in the issues of this area.”

title: ‘He hasn’t been across it’: Chris Bowen ‘hasn’t understood’ the severity of fuel crisis
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor claims Energy Minister Chris Bowen “didn’t understand” there was a fuel problem.   “Tuesday last week … there were no problems at all, and to the extent they were, it was Australians' fault,” Mr Taylor told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “By Thursday, it was a national crisis.  “He hasn’t been across it.  “We need an energy minister who gets across the issues.”

title: ‘Labor is asleep at the wheel’: Angus Taylor blames government for ‘raging’ inflation
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor discusses the inflation rate to be decided on Tuesday.   “Our inflation in this country is homegrown,” Mr Taylor told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “It’s coming from a government who has failed to control spending.  “The result of that is that inflation is raging.  “It is destined to get worse.  “I think we are in for further rises.  “Australians are all paying the price for a government that is asleep at the wheel.”

title: ‘Do your art and shut up’: Gene Simmons lashes out at woke celebrities
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond praises KISS band member Gene Simmons for his message to woke celebrities protesting their geopolitics at the Oscars.   “Nobody is interested in your opinions, that includes me,” Mr Simmons said.   “Who the f**k do you think you are?”

title: Australian teachers want to be paid to attend school camps
description: Sky News host James Macpherson believes teachers should be paid for attending school camps.   “I agree it is a sad thing that school kids are going to miss out on camps,” Mr Macpherson said.   “I totally understand why teachers want to be paid.  “I can’t believe that teachers have agreed to go on these camps for free all these years.”

title: ‘Best they could come up with’: Media slammed for targeting premier in beer-pouring stunt
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond hits out at journalists for “going after” South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas for pulling beers without having a Responsible Service of Alcohol ticket.
